content.method: Psychometric piloting of alternative item types (e.g., untimed reasoning tasks, portfolio-based assessment) tested for coachability using randomized test-prep exposure studies; parallel legal analysis of Fundamental Law jurisprudence on equal treatment versus free school choice, referencing existing Constitutional Court precedent on analogous SES-based interventions.
content.question: Is coaching-resistant, SES-weighted admission exam design technically achievable given the documented near-perfect coachability of current tests, and would any such SES-proxy adjustment survive constitutional or political challenge under Hungary's non-negotiable free-choice framing?
content.why_it_matters: This determines whether T1 can deliver its core promise at all; if coaching resistance is technically infeasible or SES-adjustment is legally untenable, T1 collapses to a symbolic reform with no equity effect.
